content:
# Numbers Chapter 33 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 190–192 | **Source Image:** `NUM_033_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_033_page_002.jpg`, `NUM_033_page_003.jpg`
**Secondary Source:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. pa_0139–pa_0140

---

**1.** These [are] the journeys of the sons of Israel who went out from the land of Egypt by their hosts, by the hand of Moses and Aaron.

**2.** And Moses wrote their goings out according to their journeys by the mouth of YHWH; and these [are] their journeys according to their goings out.

**3.** And they journeyed from Rameses in the first month, on the fifteenth day of the first month; on the morrow of the Passover the sons of Israel went out with a high hand in the eyes of all Egypt.

**4.** And Egypt was burying [those] whom YHWH had struck among them, every firstborn; and upon their gods YHWH had executed judgments.

**5.** And the sons of Israel journeyed from Rameses and encamped at Succoth.

**6.** And they journeyed from Succoth and encamped at Etham, which [is] on the edge of the wilderness.

**7.** And they journeyed from Etham and turned back to Pi-hahiroth, which [is] before Baal-zephon, and they encamped before Migdol.

**8.** And they journeyed from before Hahiroth and crossed over through the midst of the sea into the wilderness, and they went a journey of three days in the wilderness of Etham and encamped at Marah.

**9.** And they journeyed from Marah and came to Elim; and in Elim [were] twelve springs of water and seventy palm trees, and they encamped there.

**10.** And they journeyed from Elim and encamped by the Sea of Reeds.

**11.** And they journeyed from the Sea of Reeds and encamped in the wilderness of Sin.

**12.** And they journeyed from the wilderness of Sin and encamped at Dophkah.

**13.** And they journeyed from Dophkah and encamped at Alush.

**14.** And they journeyed from Alush and encamped at Rephidim; and there was no water there for the people to drink.

**15.** And they journeyed from Rephidim and encamped in the wilderness of Sinai.

**16.** And they journeyed from the wilderness of Sinai and encamped at Kibroth-hattaavah.

**17.** And they journeyed from Kibroth-hattaavah and encamped at Hazeroth.

**18.** And they journeyed from Hazeroth and encamped at Rithmah.

**19.** And they journeyed from Rithmah and encamped at Rimmon-perez.

**20.** And they journeyed from Rimmon-perez and encamped at Libnah.

**21.** And they journeyed from Libnah and encamped at Rissah.

**22.** And they journeyed from Rissah and encamped at Kehelathah.

**23.** And they journeyed from Kehelathah and encamped at Mount Shepher.

**24.** And they journeyed from Mount Shepher and encamped at Haradah.

**25.** And they journeyed from Haradah and encamped at Makheloth.

**26.** And they journeyed from Makheloth and encamped at Tahath.

**27.** And they journeyed from Tahath and encamped at Terah.

**28.** And they journeyed from Terah and encamped at Mithkah.

**29.** And they journeyed from Mithkah and encamped at Hashmonah.

**30.** And they journeyed from Hashmonah and encamped at Moseroth.

**31.** And they journeyed from Moseroth and encamped at Bene-jaakan.

**32.** And they journeyed from Bene-jaakan and encamped at Hor-haggidgad.

**33.** And they journeyed from Hor-haggidgad and encamped at Jotbathah.

**34.** And they journeyed from Jotbathah and encamped at Abronah.

**35.** And they journeyed from Abronah and encamped at Ezion-geber.

**36.** And they journeyed from Ezion-geber and encamped in the wilderness of Zin — that [is] Kadesh.

**37.** And they journeyed from Kadesh and encamped at Mount Hor, on the edge of the land of Edom.

**38.** And Aaron the priest went up to Mount Hor by the mouth of YHWH and died there, in the fortieth year of the going out of the sons of Israel from the land of Egypt, in the fifth month, on the first [day] of the month.

**39.** And Aaron [was] one hundred and twenty-three years old at his dying on Mount Hor.

**40.** And the Canaanite, the king of Arad — and he [was] dwelling in the Negeb in the land of Canaan — heard of the coming of the sons of Israel.

**41.** And they journeyed from Mount Hor and encamped at Zalmonah.

**42.** And they journeyed from Zalmonah and encamped at Punon.

**43.** And they journeyed from Punon and encamped at Oboth.

**44.** And they journeyed from Oboth and encamped at Iye-abarim, on the border of Moab.

**45.** And they journeyed from Iyim and encamped at Dibon-gad.

**46.** And they journeyed from Dibon-gad and encamped at Almon-diblathaim.

**47.** And they journeyed from Almon-diblathaim and encamped in the mountains of Abarim, before Nebo.

**48.** And they journeyed from the mountains of Abarim and encamped in the plains of Moab, by the Jordan [at] Jericho.

**49.** And they encamped by the Jordan, from Beth-jeshimoth to Abel-shittim, in the plains of Moab.

**50.** And YHWH spoke to Moses in the plains of Moab by the Jordan [at] Jericho, saying,

**51.** "Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them: When you cross over the Jordan into the land of Canaan,

**52.** then you shall drive out all the inhabitants of the land from before you, and you shall destroy all their carved stones, and all their molten images you shall destroy, and all their high places you shall demolish.

**53.** And you shall dispossess [the inhabitants of] the land and dwell in it, for to you I have given the land to possess it.

**54.** And you shall divide the land by lot for an inheritance among your families: to the many you shall make great his inheritance, and to the few you shall make small his inheritance; wherever the lot falls to him, there it shall be his. According to the tribes of your fathers you shall inherit.

**55.** And if you do not drive out the inhabitants of the land from before you, then it shall be [that] those whom you leave of them [shall be] as pricks in your eyes and as thorns in your sides, and they shall trouble you upon the land in which you [are] dwelling.

**56.** And it shall be [that] as I purposed to do to them, I shall do to you."

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **Verse 3 — "on the morrow of the Passover" (מִמׇּחֳרַת הַפֶּסַח):** The Hebrew מׇּחֳרַת (mochorath) denotes "the morrow/next day." This is the 15th of Nisan, which is itself the first day of Unleavened Bread. The Passover lamb was slain on the 14th at twilight; the departure was on the 15th.

2. **Verse 4 — "upon their gods YHWH had executed judgments" (וּבֵאלֹהֵיהֶם עָשָׂה יְהוָה שְׁפָטִים):** The MT uses שְׁפָטִים (shephatim, "judgments"), referring to punitive acts against Egyptian deities. The LXX (Vaticanus) renders this with ἐποίησεν κύριος ἐκδίκησιν ("the Lord executed vengeance"), using a different semantic range but the same essential concept.

3. **Verse 7 — "Pi-hahiroth" (פִּי הַחִירֹת):** Literally "mouth of the gorges/channels." The LC text reads פְּנֵי הַחִירֹת in some traditions (cf. Exodus 14:2), but here the MT has פִּי. The Vaticanus reads ἐπιστραφέντες ("having turned back"), matching the Hebrew וַיָּשׇׁב.

4. **Verse 8 — "from before Hahiroth" (מִפְּנֵי הַחִירֹת):** Note the shift from פִּי (v.7) to פְּנֵי (v.8). The MT preserves this distinction: they encamped at the "mouth" of Hahiroth, then journeyed from "before" Hahiroth.

5. **Verse 8 — "wilderness of Etham" (מִדְבַּר אֵתָם):** This is also called "Shur" in Exodus 15:22. The travel itinerary here preserves the alternate name.

6. **Verse 36 — "wilderness of Zin — that [is] Kadesh" (מִדְבַּר־צִן הִוא קָדֵשׁ):** The demonstrative הִוא ("that [is]") serves as an explanatory gloss identifying the wilderness of Zin with Kadesh. This is Kadesh-barnea, the staging point from which the spies were sent (Numbers 13:26).

7. **Verse 39 — Aaron's age:** The MT gives Aaron's age as "one hundred and twenty-three years" (שָׁלֹשׁ וְעֶשְׂרִים וּמְאַת שָׁנָה). The LXX (Vaticanus) likewise reads ἑκατὸν εἴκοσι τριῶν ἐτῶν, confirming agreement on the number 123.

8. **Verse 44 — "Iye-abarim" (עִיֵּי הָעֲבָרִים):** Literally "ruins of the crossings/regions beyond." Verse 45 abbreviates this to "Iyim" (עִיִּים), the shortened form of the same place name.

9. **Verse 52 — "carved stones" (מַשְׂכִּיֹּתָם):** The Hebrew מַשְׂכִּית (maskith) refers to "figured stones" or "carved images" — stone reliefs or carved surfaces used in worship. This is distinct from פֶּסֶל (carved idol) and מַסֵּכָה (molten image), which also appear in this verse.

10. **Verse 52 — "molten images" (צַלְמֵי מַסֵּכֹתָם):** Literally "images of their castings." The construct chain pairs צֶלֶם (image/likeness) with מַסֵּכָה (molten/cast thing), referring to metal cult statues.

11. **Verse 52 — "high places" (בָּמוֹתָם):** The בָּמָה (bamah) is an elevated cultic platform. The command to "demolish" (תְּשַׁמִּידוּ, from שׁמד) these is absolute — the hiphil imperative demands complete destruction.

12. **Verse 54 — Lot inheritance:** The Hebrew uses the hiphil of רבה ("make great") and מעט ("make small") for proportional land allocation. The larger tribe receives proportionally more territory; the smaller tribe, less. Yet the lot (גּוֹרָל) determines the specific location.

13. **Verse 55 — "pricks in your eyes and thorns in your sides" (לְשִׂכִּים בְּעֵינֵיכֶם וְלִצְנִינִם בְּצִדֵּיכֶם):** The שֵׂכ (sek) denotes a sharp barb or thorn-prick. The צְנִינִים (tseninnim) are thorns or spines. Both metaphors describe persistent, painful irritation — the remaining Canaanite populations will be a continual affliction.

14. **Verses 5–49 — Itinerary formula:** The chapter preserves a highly formulaic travel log using the repeated pattern וַיִּסְעוּ מִן...וַיַּחֲנוּ בְּ ("And they journeyed from...and encamped at..."). This is one of the most systematically structured passages in the Torah. The 42 stations from Rameses to the plains of Moab have been much discussed in rabbinic and scholarly literature.

15. **Vaticanus agreement:** The LXX text in Codex Vaticanus (pa_0139–pa_0140) follows the same itinerary sequence and station count as the MT, with minor orthographic variations in transliterated place names (e.g., Ρεμεσση for Rameses, Σοκχωθ for Succoth). No significant divergences in content or verse order were observed.
